notes: The doctrinally load-bearing terms are yadṛcchayā (unsought / by chance), upapannam (arrived), svarga-dvāram apāvṛtam (door of heaven wide open), and sukhinaḥ (happy / fortunate). The engineering layer preserves all four. yadṛcchayā is correctly anchored as the operative word — 'arrived unsought into the operator's lane,' 'a problem one chases is not the verse,' and the Sanskrit phrase appears in the concrete_scenario. The svarga-dvāra-apāvṛtam (the unobstructed gate) is mapped to the 'gate has opened and walking through it' framing — preserving the directness emphasis Vishvanatha names ('without obstructions, apāvṛtam'). The sukhinaḥ (happy / fortunate) is preserved as descriptive (the 'lucky operator is the one in front of whom such a problem opens'), not prescriptive (no exhortation to feel happy). The Ramanuja reading (unsought-arrival as evidence of accumulated merit) is honoured in the analog's 'arrival is itself information.' Critical preservation: the militarism misreading is explicitly avoided — the verse is anchored in the unsought-arrival of dharma-yuddha, not in seeking battle. PASS.
